title: Oregon Trail Wagon Train (1843)
description: Highly simplified route of a typical wagon train from Independence, Missouri to the Willamette Valley in 1843.

moments.title: Departure from Independence
moments.body: Families gather wagons, livestock, and supplies before heading west into the unknown.

moments.title: Crossing the Rockies
moments.body: At South Pass, wagons cross the Continental Divide before the snows close the route.

moments.title: Snake River Country
moments.body: Dust, heat, and river crossings test the wagons and the people driving them.

moments.title: Reaching the Willamette
moments.body: After months on the trail, the wagon train descends into Oregon's fertile valleys.

conditions.label: Forming the wagon train and leaving Independence
Crossing the plains toward the Platte River
Along the Platte and North Platte, steady travel
conditions.weather: Hot and dusty with summer storms

conditions.label: Climbing into the Rockies and South Pass
Across the dry stretches and Snake River crossings
conditions.weather: Dry, windy days and chilly nights

conditions.label: Final push through the Columbia toward Oregon City

attribution: Living Historical Journeys — mechanically ported from the proof of concept.

schedule.label: Approaching South Pass in the Rockies
